A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
Why is Not Grace Given According to Merit?
originalBook: A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
title: Why is Not Grace Given According to Merit?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Chapter 16 [VIII.]—Why is Not Grace Given According to Merit?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But “why,” says one, “is not the grace of God given according to men’s merits?”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: I answer, Because God is merciful.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: “Why, then,” it is asked, “is it not given to all?”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And here I reply, Because God is a Judge.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And thus grace is given by Him freely; and by His righteous judgment it is shown in some what grace confers on those to whom it is given.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Let us not then be ungrateful, that according to the good pleasure of His will a merciful God delivers so many to the praise of the glory of His grace from such deserved perdition; as, if He should deliver no one therefrom, He would not be unrighteous.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Let him, therefore, who is delivered love His grace.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Let him who is not delivered acknowledge his due.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: If, in remitting a debt, goodness is perceived, in requiring it, justice—unrighteousness is never found to be with God.
